summary: A couple of years ago, when I was a junior in high school, I went on a mission trip for leadership with my youth group down in New Orleans. While we were at the hotel there, a lady approached me and greeted me with 'Aloha!' which really surprised me because I had never seen her before. That moment stuck with me because it challenged my sense of identity and inspired me to write a poem about being Hmong and embracing my culture. New Orleans was the place where this important experience happened, marking a turning point in how I see myself.

Two years ago, I had my first poem published in the Saint Paul Almanac, which was a huge milestone for me as a Hmong American writer. Saint Paul is where my work gained recognition beyond local coffee shops and open mics, and it felt like a proud moment in my journey of reclaiming my Hmong identity. This city represents a place where I could share my story and culture through my poetry, connecting with others and inspiring myself to keep writing. Saint Paul holds a special place in my creative and personal growth.
